channel.title: Areas on Vishpala
channel.description: Recent content in Areas on Vishpala

item.title: Accessibility
item.description:
Accessibility is not a checklist or a compliance exercise. It is the practice of building things that work for the full range of people who need to use them — across abilities, devices, languages, and connection speeds.

We approach accessibility as a discipline that runs through every stage of a project, not a phase that happens after design and development are complete. The cost of retrofitting accessibility is always higher than building it in from the start.

item.title: Design
item.description:
We practice design as a discipline of clarity. Our work begins with the people who will use it — their actual contexts, access levels, languages, and needs — and builds outward from there toward visual form, not the other way around.

Design at Vishpala is inseparable from accessibility. We treat WCAG 2.2 Level AA conformance as a floor, not a ceiling, and we test with real people across a range of abilities and assistive technologies at every stage of a project.

item.title: Infrastructure
item.description:
We design and build infrastructure that organisations can own outright — not in the legal sense only, but in the operational sense. Systems they can understand, document, rebuild, and hand off without asking permission from a vendor.

Our infrastructure practice covers the full stack from physical hosting decisions through to deployment pipelines, monitoring, backup, and the documentation that makes it all maintainable by a human being at two in the morning.
